enforced.oracle: Parsing the original VCF and the VCF with shuffled meta-information lines (except ##fileformat pinned first) must produce identical canonical JSON output. The biological semantics are unchanged because the spec allows any order of meta-information lines.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key=value field ordering invariance
enforced.oracle: Reordering key=value pairs inside ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER structured meta-lines must produce identical canonical JSON output. The spec mandates that implementations must not rely on field ordering within structured lines.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Reject fileformat not on first line
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to parse) a file where ##fileformat is not the first line; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Reject GT index out of bounds
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to parse) a record where a GT allele index exceeds the number of defined ALT alleles (0 for REF, 1..len(ALT) for alternates); silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to parse) a record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a value count that does not equal the number of ALT alleles; silent acceptance is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
The Number entry is an Integer that describes the number of values that can be included with the INFO field.

enforced.mr_name: POS coordinate invariance under meta-line shuffling with query_method_roundtrip
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method. Shuffling meta-lines does not alter record coordinates or variant type classification.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1. Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle invariance for variant query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — meta-information lines other than ##fileformat may appear in any order per spec, so shuffling them must not alter any parsed record's query-method return values.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field order permutation invariance for variant query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured meta-information lines, so permuting key=value pairs must not change parsed record query results.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key=value ordering invariance in data lines
quarantine.oracle: Shuffling the semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column of data records must produce identical canonical JSON output. The spec does not mandate any ordering of INFO sub-fields; keys are identified by their ID, not position.

evidence.quote: The exact format of each INFO key should be specified in the meta-information (as described above). Example of a complete INFO field: DP=154;MQ=52;H2.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs may appear in any order, but the example shows an arbitrary order and no ordering rule is defined. The SHOULD in 'should be specified' is advisory.

quarantine.oracle: Permuting the sample columns (header IDs and all data rows together) must produce identical canonical JSON output when samples are accessed by ID. The spec identifies samples by column header name, not by column position.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering. The header line format (#CHROM POS ID REF ALT QUAL FILTER INFO FORMAT SAMPLE1 SAMPLE2 ...) is shown in examples but no normative rule mandates sample column order. Sample identity is by column header name.

quarantine.mr_name: BCF header dictionary ordering invariance via round-trip
quarantine.oracle: Shuffling the order of ##contig, ##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER dictionary entries, round-tripping through BCF and back to VCF, must produce identical canonical JSON output. BCF codec re-indexes dictionary entries so order is implementation-defined.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order. Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.

ambiguity_flags: The BCF dictionary ordering rule (VCF v4.5 §6.2.1) was not retrieved within threshold distance. The meta-information lines rule (p122) confirms any order is allowed for VCF meta-lines, and BCF must have all entries present.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of dependent fields
quarantine.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and remapping GT indices and Number=A/R fields accordingly, the parsed variant semantics (allele identities, genotype calls) remain equivalent. P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all query methods.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
AF		    A		    Float		    Allele frequency for each ALT allele in the same order as listed (estimated from primary data, not called genotypes)

quarantine.mr_name: ALT permutation invariance of query methods on parsed records
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method, since ALT permutation with consistent remapping preserves the underlying variant semantics.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
LAA		    .			    Integer	    1-based indices into ALT, indicating which alleles are relevant (local) for the current sample

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the canonical normalized representation must be semantically identical; any spec-compliant parser must recognize the trimmed form as the same variant as the untrimmed form. The variant's biological meaning (allele type, genomic position after left-anchoring) is preserved.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity means two parsers could disagree on whether to trim common affixes, but the biological variant is the same either way.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — left-shifting an indel within a homopolymer run produces a canonical representation of the same biological variant. A spec-compliant parser must recognize both representations as equivalent.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly mandate left-alignment of indels in homopolymer runs; this is a convention from Tan et al. 2015. The padding base rule permits but does not require the minimal representation.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — round-tripping VCF through BCF2 binary encoding and back must produce semantically identical canonical output. Per VCF v4.5 §6, BCF is a binary encoding of the same data model; any difference exposes a codec bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: BCF encoding of missing values and zero-length vectors may differ from VCF text representation; the spec notes 'BCF encodes zero length vectors as MISSING' which could cause ambiguity.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — parsing a VCF file with the primary SUT, then re-serializing via its public writer API, then parsing again must produce identical canonical output. Any diff exposes a writer bug in the primary SUT.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The SUT's writer may normalize representations (e.g., trim common affixes, left-align indels) during serialization, which could cause parse(write(parse(x))) != parse(x) even though both are valid canonical forms.

quarantine.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to parse) a data line missing one of the 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO); silent acceptance or auto-padding is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: #CHROM     POS     ID     REF     ALT     QUAL     FILTER     INFO     FORMAT     SAMPLE1
Zero length fields are not allowed, a dot (``.'') must be used instead.

quarantine.mr_name: Missing value round-trip invariance via equivalent FORMAT fields
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T appends a FORMAT field filled with '.' to all samples; missing values are semantically equivalent to absent trailing fields

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] If any of the fields is missing, it is replaced with the MISSING value. ... Trailing fields can be dropped, with the exception of the GT field, which should always be present if specified in the FORMAT field.

[VCF v4.5 — Data lines] If a field contains a list of missing values, it can be represented either as a single MISSING value (`.') or as a list of missing values (e.g. `.,.,.' if the field was Number=3).

ambiguity_flags: The spec says GT 'should always be present' (SHOULD, not MUST), so a parser that drops GT when missing may be non-compliant but some implementations might differ

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle invariance for variant query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — the INFO column's semicolon-separated key=value pairs have no prescribed order, so shuffling them must not alter any parsed record's query-method return values.

evidence.quote: Clarified INFO lines field ordering
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT allele (with synchronized Number=A/R arrays and remapped GT indices) must produce the same set of variants. A spec-compliant parser must treat the split form as semantically identical to the multi-ALT form.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
The first key must always be the genotype (GT) if it is present.
